eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eely: adjective. Resembling an eel: long, thin and slippery.
elm: noun. A tree of the genus Ulmus of the family Ulmaceae, large deciduous trees with alternate stipulate leaves and small apetalous flowers.

elope: verb. Of a married or engaged person, to run away from home with a paramour.
employ: verb. To retain (someone) as an employee.

epee: noun. A sharp-pointed dueling sword with a bell-shaped guard, used (with the end blunted) in sport fencing.
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.

mel: noun. A unit of pitch on a scale of pitches perceived by listeners to be equally spaced from one another.
melee: noun. A battle fought at close range, one not involving ranged weapons; hand-to-hand combat; brawling.
mole: noun. A naevus, a pigmented, slightly raised, and sometimes hairy spot on the skin.
moly: noun. A magic herb or plant used by Odysseus to overcome Circe.
mop: noun. An implement for washing floors or similar, made of a piece of cloth, or a collection of thrums, or coarse yarn, fastened to a handle.
mope: verb. To carry oneself in a depressed, lackadaisical manner; to give oneself up to low spirits; to pout, sulk.
mopey: adjective. Given to moping; in a depressed condition, low in spirits; lackadaisical.
ole: interjection. An interjection used to stir up excitement.
ope: interjection. An exclamation of surprise; oops.

ploy: noun. A tactic, strategy, or scheme.
poem: noun. A literary piece written in verse.

pom: noun. An Englishman; a Briton; a person of British descent.
pome: noun. A type of fruit in which the often edible flesh arises from the swollen base of the flower and not from the carpels.
yelp: noun. An abrupt, high-pitched noise or utterance.

yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
emo: noun. Any form of guitar - driven alternative rock that is particularly or notably emotional; a broadly encompassing genre of rock music
lem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomaande.
leme: noun. A ray or glimmer of light; a gleam.
loy: noun. A type of one-eared spade used in Ireland.
lyme: verb. to hang out (to spend time doing nothing in particular)
mele: noun. A chant in Polynesia, especially Hawaii, typically in praise of a leader or to commemorate some significant event.

olpe: noun. Originally, a leather flask or vessel for oils or liquids; afterward, an earthenware vase or pitcher without a spout.
peele: noun. A kind of antelope, the rhebok, Pelea capreolus.
yee: pronoun. You (the people being addressed); alternative spelling of ye.
yelm: noun. A bundle of straw laid out straight, chiefly to be used for thatching; a helm.
ympe: noun. Obsolete form of imp (“young shoot of a plant”).
